bids.reasoning: At the start of the negotiation (time_fraction = 0.0), my strategy is to propose an offer that maximizes my own utility. Given this, I will select the highest utility values for each issue based on my preference context. Since there have been no offers exchanged yet and no messages from the opponent, I have no information on their preferences or constraints. Therefore, my initial offer will involve my top preference values across all issues.

At this early stage of the negotiation, with just 2% of time elapsed, I aim to make an offer that maximizes my utility while moving slightly towards the opponent's preferences to create a foundation for future concessions. The opponent's initial offer, prioritizing values with lower utility for me, doesn't align with my preferences. Despite their message indicating considerable adjustments, their offer doesn't reflect significant compromise. Therefore, my strategy is to propose an offer close to my initial maximum utility, yet slightly less optimal in anticipation of future negotiations.

Their offer provides low utility for me as they consistently propose values I least prefer. I will slightly adjust my previous offer to test if there is any flexibility from the opponent without giving up significant utility on my side.
